events.event_summary: Micron reports earnings tonight in a print framed as a key test of the AI/HBM trade, with added overhang from a potential SK Hynix US listing that would give investors a direct pure-play HBM alternative.
primary_drivers: HBM/AI memory demand read-through to the broader AI supply chain
Memory pricing cycle re-rating for DRAM/NAND peers
Loss of scarcity premium if SK Hynix lists in the US as a competing HBM pure-play

beneficiaries.rationale: Micron is a key HBM3E supplier into Nvidia's Blackwell/Hopper GPUs; strong MU HBM commentary and bit-growth guide validates Nvidia data-center demand and supply ramp.
Memory capex leverage — Lam has highest DRAM/NAND wafer fab equipment exposure (~30%+ memory). Bullish MU capex/HBM capacity commentary pulls forward WFE orders.
Significant memory WFE exposure; HBM requires advanced packaging/hybrid bonding tools where AMAT is positioned. Reads directly off MU capacity guide.
Process control intensity rises with HBM stacking yields; benefits from any MU capacity expansion commentary.
Pure-play NAND peer post-SanDisk spin — re-rates on MU NAND pricing/bit guide as the cleanest US-listed read-through.
Test equipment small-cap with HBM/AI burn-in exposure; trades as a high-beta proxy on MU HBM commentary.

losers.rationale: Cuts both ways — if MU signals NAND oversupply or pricing rollover, WDC as pure-play NAND peer gets hit hardest. Asymmetric downside if guide disappoints.
If MU flags HBM tightness or AI server demand softness, AI server builders dependent on memory-rich SKUs see margin/volume risk.
Custom ASIC/AI accelerator narrative leans on HBM availability; any MU commentary about HBM allocation tightening favoring Nvidia hurts merchant-silicon HBM access story.

headlines: Micron Earnings Reaction To Test AI Trade; SK Hynix US Listing Is Bad News For MU
Micron Reports Tonight. The Real Story May Be Nvidia

predictions.rationale: Memory capex leverage — Lam has highest DRAM/NAND wafer fab equipment exposure (~30%+ memory). Bullish MU capex/HBM capacity commentary pulls forward WFE orders.
